hypothesis: Under varying ionic strength, a Debye-length dependent crossover in polymer–surface adhesion emerges, whereby polymer motifs that maximize strong-bead–surface contacts promote adsorption at low salt but become less favorable at higher salt due to electrostatic screening; a Debye-length–augmented CNN–GRU–Transformer model trained on salt-diverse coarse-grained simulations will generalize to predict this crossover for unseen surface patterns.
